market_breadth.nyse_advance_decline: Hit new high on May 7; narrow leadership since mid-April
market_breadth.nyse_new_highs: Expanding on NASDAQ; little changed on NYSE

cnn_fear_greed.historical_context: Near neutral-to-greedy boundary; index ticked down from 67 to 64 this week
cnn_fear_greed.notes: Sentiment has moderated from extreme optimism

vix_analysis.notes: VIX has contracted from mid-20s highs; complacency rising but not extreme

aaii_sentiment.contrarian_signal: Neutral-Bullish; bulls above historical average but not extreme
aaii_sentiment.notes: Retail bulls remain above 37.5% historical average; inching up to 39.3%

naaim_exposure.interpretation: Active managers pulled back sharply from 96.67% to 77.34%; rapid de-risking
naaim_exposure.contrarian_reading: Bullish reversal signal - managers reduced exposure at all-time highs
naaim_exposure.notes: NAAIM dropped 19+ points in one week. Most bearish response hit -200%. Median remains 99%.

equity_only.notes: Extremely low equity P/C signals excessive call buying / speculative euphoria

cboe_put_call.notes: Equity-only PCR at 0.59 is contrarian bearish warning. Index PCR at 1.03 shows institutional hedging.

margin_debt.contrarian_reading: Margin contraction reduces systemic risk; some deleveraging underway
margin_debt.notes: FINRA margin debt fell to $1.221T in March from $1.253T; second consecutive monthly decline

high_yield_spread.notes: HYG OAS at 282 bps; elevated relative to bull market norms but not crisis level

investment_advisors.notes: II survey data not available for current week

institutional_fund_flows.notes: BofA FMS shows highest cash since May 2025 and least equity OW since July 2025

sentimen_trader.smart_dumb_classification: Below -0.25 - Dumb Money more bearish than Smart Money
sentimen_trader.notes: Spread rapidly cycled from +0.25 (extreme optimism) to -0.27 in ~31 trading days. Rapid reversals at record peaks historically precede difficult outcomes. S&P 500 at all-time highs.

yield_curve.notes: 10Y-2Y at +50 bps; curve re-steepened. TYFI fragility index at 52/100 (HIGH).

contrarian_signals.notes: Balanced sentiment per contrariansignals.com; watch for extreme readings

contrarian_analysis.overall_contrarian_reading: Caution - Rapid sentiment reversal at all-time highs with low equity hedging

vix.reading: VIX at 21 is elevated relative to SPX at all-time highs; suggests some underlying anxiety despite record prices

cboe_pcr.reading: Equity-only PCR at 0.59 is extremely low, indicating speculative call buying euphoria. Index PCR at 1.03 shows smart money is hedging.

aaii.reading: Bulls at 39.3% above historical average of 37.5%. Not extreme but elevated. Bears at 36.6% are relatively high, creating a narrow spread.

naaim.reading: NAAIM plunged from 96.67 to 77.34 in one week. Most bearish response hit -200%. Active managers aggressively reduced equity exposure at all-time highs. Historically, such rapid de-risking after peak exposure has created short-term volatility but medium-term buying opportunities.

margin_debt.reading: Margin debt declined 2.55% to $1.221T. Deleveraging reduces systemic vulnerability and creates future buying power.

fear_greed.reading: Fear & Greed at 64 is in 'Greed' territory but well below extreme levels (>75). Index has moderated from 67 to 64 this week.

smart_dumb_spread.reading: Spread cycled from +0.25 extreme optimism to -0.27 in just 31 trading days. Rapid sentiment reversals at record peaks historically led to difficult outcomes. S&P 500 at all-time highs with dumb money now more bearish than smart money.

bofa_fms.reading: Fund managers at most bearish sentiment since June 2025. Cash at 4.3% (highest since May 2025). Underweight equities -59% (least since July 2025). Stagflation fears dominate. BofA itself notes these are contrarian buy signals if oil falls below $84.

contrarian_analysis.composite_signal: Caution - Mixed signals with high warning readings from PCR and Smart/Dumb spread, offset by bearish institutional sentiment creating contrarian buy setup
contrarian_analysis.notes: The most notable development is the rapid sentiment cycle: NAAIM collapsed 19 points in one week, and Smart/Dumb spread reversed from extreme optimism to pessimism in just 31 days while SPX sits at all-time highs. Historically this pattern preceded tactical pullbacks.

relevant_policies.impact: Moderate - ongoing trade negotiations create headline risk
FOMC on hold; 10Y at 4.59% reflects elevated rate expectations
High - 76% of fund managers expect stagflation per BofA FMS
relevant_policies.market_reaction: Flight to quality vs cyclical rotation ongoing

relevant_policies.impact: High - Iran ceasefire hopes could send oil below $84
relevant_policies.market_reaction: Energy sector volatile; $100/bbl Brent creating inflation anxiety

policy_cross_impact.policy_notes: Stagflation is the dominant macro fear. Fund managers extremely bearish on growth and inflation simultaneously. This is a classic late-cycle setup.

dashboard.notes: Dashboard shows mixed extremes: equity PCR at 15th percentile (speculative), BofA FMS at 20th percentile (bearish institutions), Smart/Dumb at 25th percentile (rapid reversal).

key_events.impact: Watch for follow-through on Friday's late-week price action
Housing data will inform growth/stagflation narrative
Key insight into Fed thinking on tariff-inflation tradeoff

sentiment_thresholds.vix_watch: Watch for VIX breakout above 24 to confirm anxiety
sentiment_thresholds.pcr_watch: Equity PCR below 0.55 would be extreme speculation
sentiment_thresholds.fear_greed_watch: Break below 55 would signal sentiment shift
sentiment_thresholds.naaim_watch: Further drop below 70 would confirm manager de-risking

tomorrow_watch.contrarian_setup: Rapid sentiment reversal at all-time highs creates elevated tactical pullback risk. However, extremely bearish institutional positioning (BofA FMS) and margin deleveraging create contrarian buy-the-dip support. Watch for VIX spike above 24 + PCR collapse below 0.55 as confirmation of near-term top. Target support 7300-7200 on any pullback.
tomorrow_watch.notes: The week ahead is dominated by FOMC minutes (Wed) and ongoing tariff/trade headlines. The rapid sentiment reversal is the key watch item.
